title: psoralea glabra
intro: Psoralea glabra, also known as Indian turnip or goat's bread, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of India and the Himalayas. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system. The health benefits of Psoralea glabra include its potential to improve digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments such as fever and skin conditions. Psoralea glabra can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
